title: coronilla minima var. fruticans
intro: Coronilla minima var. fruticans, also known as the small crown-chafer or dwarf coronilla, is a small, perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and acting as a diuretic. Coronilla minima var. fruticans is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and it can also be used in the form of tinctures or ointments to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
